pages.raw_text:
At the meeting of the retail clerks
union held Monday evening at Central
Labor hall no action was taken in
the matter of the formation of a
lady clerks union, although it was
understood that decisive steps in this
direction were to be made.


The question of the closing of
stores on Sunday was discussed at
some length, and it was found that
in order to bring the movement to a
successful issue it would be neces-
sary to receive the cooperation of
the authorities. Several new men,
bers were initiated, and 1t was re-
solved on account of the increasing
strength in membership to secure
another hall, as the one now used is
not commodious enough and is not
centrally situated.


All branches of work in England
stop work at noon on Saturdays.


On the first of the year there was
general raise in wages in San
Francisco.


The plumbers, laundry workers
and machinists will meet tonight at
Central Labor hall.


The municipal ownership of fran
chises has been declared by the Cen
trail Labor union of Erie, Pa.


A convention was held at Everett,
Wash, for the purpose of organic
ing a national single weavers' asso-
citation.


1t is expected that the Georgia
child labor bill will be defeated when
it comes to vote in the lower house
of the legislature.


A recent law passed in New York
places an employer in jail for s days
if he refuses to pay a female en-
ploye wages when work is done.
The Bartenders' league initiated
six new members at the meeting held
last night, which raises the member
ship roll to 1876.


Tom Heskett will probably replace
Horace Brown as secretary of the
trades council, as Mr.. Brown is I'M
able TO attend to the duties of the
office on account of business which
demands his presence elsewhere on
Tuesday evenings.


The builders and architects petit
| ton was read before the trades
council last night. The petition re-
quested the council to use it's infu-
ence against the hodcarriers' demand
for an increase of wages from $3.50
to 81 per. day, but the council de-
| cided not to take any action on the
matter until the next meeting.
